_meta.title: CUA Faculty Constellation — ExI Workshop Preparation
_meta.description: Faculty at CUA Columbus School of Law whose scholarship intersects with Exclusive Inclusion theory. For workshop preparation April 13, 2026.

nodes.label: Exclusive Inclusion
nodes.author: Oranburg
nodes.desc: Your paper. Governance architecture produces systematic identity-based exclusion.

nodes.label: Breger
nodes.author: Marshall J. Breger
nodes.desc: MOST URGENT. Active on campus antisemitism. 25+ law review articles. May challenge structural diagnosis with cultural explanation. Read his Moment pieces.

nodes.label: Colinvaux
nodes.author: Roger Colinvaux
nodes.desc: Nonprofit tax expert. Already cited FN 108 (incomplete!). Will engage Part IV operational test from tax-law angle. ABA Outstanding Academic Award 2024.

nodes.label: Miles
nodes.author: Veryl V. Miles
nodes.desc: Already cited FN 107. Second article in CUA Law Review on mission specificity in faith-based law schools — YOUR argument applied to THEIR institution.

nodes.label: Destro
nodes.author: Robert A. Destro
nodes.desc: Faculty since 1982. Church autonomy, civil rights. Will press on Title VI enforcement vs governance architecture distinction.

nodes.label: DeGirolami
nodes.author: Marc O. DeGirolami
nodes.desc: Newman Chair. 'Disentangling Conscience and Religion' — vocabulary overlap with YOUR disentanglement matrix. Prepare for productive tension.

nodes.label: Alicea
nodes.author: J. Joel Alicea
nodes.desc: Assoc Dean for Faculty Research. First CUA in Yale Law Journal. Originalist — will scrutinize constitutional argument in Part IV.

nodes.label: Silecchia
nodes.author: Lucia A. Silecchia
nodes.desc: Assoc Dean. Guardianship theory from Catholic social thought — parallels hollow duty of obedience. Shapes institutional research culture.

nodes.label: Walsh
nodes.author: Kevin C. Walsh
nodes.desc: Knights of Columbus Chair. Federal judicial power, Thomistic juridical realism. Relevant to standing reform.

nodes.label: Rienzi
nodes.author: Mark L. Rienzi
nodes.desc: Center for Religious Liberty. Already cited FN 109. Represented students in 2024 UCLA antisemitism case — live litigation in ExI's territory.

nodes.label: Buckles
nodes.author: Johnny Rex Buckles
nodes.desc: Already cited FN 68. Duty of loyalty for charity fiduciaries. Published in CUA Law Review.

nodes.label: Part I: Structural Condition
nodes.desc: Defines exclusive inclusion, documents across 5 identity groups, introduces disentanglement matrix.

nodes.label: Part II: Governance Void
nodes.desc: Sovereign charity framework: self-perpetuating board, hollow duty of obedience, absent stakeholder standing.

nodes.label: Part III: Federal Intervention
nodes.desc: 2025 settlements as case study — compliance without governance reform.

nodes.label: Part IV: Three Reforms
nodes.desc: Stakeholder standing, mission specificity, operational test reform.
